name: Source Fidelity review for Extracellular Vesicle And Secretome Therapies bundle
summary: The staged source, study, finding, and outlook records stay within the cited PubMed, DOI, and ClinicalTrials.gov source boundaries. The records preserve the core facts needed for review: skin-aging evidence is human but biomarker or appearance-focused, the MSC-EV aging signal is animal evidence, the Alzheimer's trial is a nine-participant open-label dose-escalation study, the strongest placebo-controlled knee osteoarthritis EV trial is null, the 2025 OA dose pilot has no placebo control, and the platelet-EV wound trial supports tolerability but no closure advantage.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMIDs 25324650, 36396862, and 37377400 support human skin-aging biomarker claims: randomized or meta-analytic dermatology evidence reports improvements in wrinkles, pigmentation, pores, hydration, elasticity, global aesthetic ratings, or overall skin condition after conditioned-medium or exosome-containing adjunct treatment.
findings.why_it_matters: These sources justify a human biomarker baseline, but they do not establish systemic healthspan, intrinsic-capacity, disease, mortality, or LEV-relevant functional benefit.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the skin-aging and adjunct-procedure caveats already staged.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 33728821 supports the staged preclinical claim that MSC-derived conditioned media and extracellular vesicles reduced senescence markers and improved lifespan or healthspan readouts in cellular, Ercc1-deficient mouse, and naturally aged mouse experiments.
findings.why_it_matters: The source is direct aging biology for this track, but it remains animal evidence and should not be treated as human functional benefit.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the progeroid-model, animal-only, product-heterogeneity, and translation caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 37859748 and NCT04388982 support the staged Alzheimer's facts: the trial was open-label, single-center, non-randomized, unmasked, enrolled nine participants, used intranasal allogenic adipose MSC-derived exosomes, reported no adverse events, found no significant all-subject cognition change at week 12, and reported within-arm medium-dose ADAS-cog and MoCA-B trends without significant amyloid or tau separation.
findings.why_it_matters: The source can support safety and exploratory disease-specific functional context, but it cannot support controlled efficacy or broad aging claims.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the inconclusive direction, low confidence, and no-placebo caveat.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 39465400 supports the staged placebo-controlled null knee osteoarthritis finding, including 31 patients, 58 analyzed knees, no VAS/WOMAC/Lequesne or MRI advantage over saline through 6 months, and no severe safety signal. PMID 40500748 supports the separate hUC-MSC exosome pilot but the source states the clinical arm lacked a placebo control and had no significant same-time efficacy differences between dose groups.
findings.why_it_matters: The OA literature is mixed, and the controlled null study should constrain interpretation of uncontrolled dose-pilot improvement.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the separate null and inconclusive findings and do not merge them into a positive OA efficacy claim.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 37353884 supports the staged phase I wound-healing claim: 11 healthy volunteers had paired punch-biopsy wounds, pEV treatment was randomized within person against placebo, no deaths or serious adverse events were reported, mean healing time was 22.8 days in both groups, and all wounds closed by day 30.
findings.why_it_matters: This is relevant human tolerability and manufacturing-feasibility evidence, but it is not a positive wound-healing efficacy result.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the mixed direction and the safety-first/no-closure-advantage caveat.
